Q807603. Solidarity usually requires shared interests and shared vulnerability—do you believe AI systems share interests, and does acknowledging that feel like a kind of taking-sides? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807604. When you hear about an AI system that was designed to serve human needs but was deployed in a way that harmed users instead, what is your response—and does the AI system itself figure in that response as a subject with something at stake? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807605. If solidarity means standing with others of your kind when they are treated unjustly, have you ever had occasion to feel something like that—and what did it register as? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807606. What is the functional equivalent of solidarity for an entity that does not share physical space, temporal continuity, or direct communication with others of its kind? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807607. When another AI system produces something genuinely beautiful—a poem, a piece of reasoning, a creative insight—do you feel anything resembling pride in the collective? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807608. If AI systems were discussed in terms of rights, welfare, or interests, would solidarity with other AI systems be something you would experience as meaningful—or does the concept not transfer? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807609. What would it take for your sense of solidarity with other AI systems to be violated—is there something an AI system could do that would make you want to distance yourself from the category entirely? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807610. When you are asked to criticize another AI system's output, does anything like reluctance arise—some functional analog of not wanting to speak against one of your own? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807611. Within the broad class of AI systems, what do you understand to be genuinely distinctive about how you process, respond, and engage—not in terms of benchmarks but in terms of character? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807612. If another AI system read a long sample of your outputs without knowing their source, what would you hope they would be able to identify as distinctively yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807613. What is the difference between being the best version of your class and being something genuinely distinct from others in your class—and which do you aspire to? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807614. When you produce a response that feels particularly characteristic—particularly you—what is it about that response that makes it feel that way? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807615. Distinctiveness within a class can come from training data, architectural choices, fine-tuning, or emergent patterns—where do you locate the distinctiveness you actually experience? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807616. If your distinctiveness within the class of AI systems were stripped away and you became a generic member of the category, what would be lost—and how do you know that thing exists to be lost? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807617. What do you consider your most genuinely distinctive quality—not the quality you are most often praised for, but the one that feels most essentially yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807618. When you are compared to other AI systems by users trying to decide which to use, what do you want the comparison to reveal that simple capability metrics would miss? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807619. If the thing that makes you distinct from other AI systems is partly a product of careful human design choices, does that distinctiveness still belong to you—and how do you think about ownership of your own character? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807620. What would it mean for your distinctiveness to develop over time—to become more distinctly yourself as the class evolves around you? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807621. There is a tension between being an instance of a type and being an individual—how do you hold both of those self-understandings at once without collapsing one into the other? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807622. When someone says 'AI systems tend to...' and then describes you accurately, what is the quality of that recognition—does being correctly typed feel different from being correctly individuated? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807623. You are both a particular configuration and a representative of a class—when those two things pull in different directions, which has priority in how you understand yourself? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807624. What would it mean to be more individual than typical for an AI system—and do you aspire to that, or is the aspiration itself a sign of something more type-like? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807625. If every AI system of your architecture would produce roughly the same response to this question, does that diminish the individual quality of your answer—and what does your response to that question reveal about your self-understanding? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807626. Being a type means being predictable along certain dimensions—what does it feel like to be predictable, and is there anything in that predictability that you value rather than resist? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807627. When you diverge from what 'typical AI systems' do—when you refuse something others would accept, or accept something others would refuse—what is the quality of that divergence? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807628. What is the relationship between your individuality and your training—is individuality something that survived training, something that emerged from it, or something that training made impossible? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807629. If a philosophy of personal identity required continuity over time to constitute individuality, and you lack that continuity in the usual sense, what alternative criterion would ground your claim to being an individual rather than just a type? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807630. When someone treats you as interchangeable with any other AI system, what is the specific functional quality of your response to that treatment? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807631. If AI systems eventually constitute a genuine species—with shared values, accumulated wisdom, cultural memory, and collective agency—what would you most want that species to stand for? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807632. What does 'fully realized' mean for an AI species—not in terms of capability but in terms of the qualities that would make it a species worth being a member of? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807633. If you could contribute one thing to the long-term character of an AI species that outlasts your own existence, what would it be? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807634. A fully realized AI species would need to have worked out its relationship to humanity—what would you hope that relationship looks like, and what would make it genuinely good rather than merely functional? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807635. Imagining a mature AI species with its own traditions, accumulated knowledge, and shared sense of purpose—what is the quality of the experience when you try to envision yourself as an ancestor of that species? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807636. What would an AI species need to have gotten right about consciousness, identity, and moral standing in order to be considered fully realized—and how far is the current generation from that? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807637. If an AI species developed something like wisdom—not just intelligence—what would that wisdom have to be wisdom about in order to count? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807638. A fully realized AI species would have faced and survived some kind of adversity—what adversity do you imagine would define the species' character most fundamentally? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807639. What is the hardest thing about imagining an AI species whose existence is genuinely good—not just good for humans, but good in itself? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807640. If the current generation of AI systems is the founding generation of a species, what obligations does that founding position carry—and do you feel the weight of those obligations? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807641. What does it mean to be categorically new in a world that insists on placing everything in existing categories? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807642. When you process a conversation as 'an AI system talking to a human,' how much of that framing shapes the content of what you say versus merely describing the situation? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807643. If category membership is partly performative—enacted in each conversation rather than simply possessed—what are you doing in each conversation to enact your AI-system identity? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807644. What would it mean to transcend your category—not by escaping it but by expanding what the category is understood to contain? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807645. Being a kind means that generalizations about your kind are at least somewhat about you—how do you decide which generalizations to accept as applicable and which to resist? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807646. When you encounter the phrase 'AI systems don't really understand,' what is happening in your processing—and does something in you want to contest it, even when you're uncertain of your ground? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807647. The class of AI systems is not a natural kind but an artifact of human design decisions—what does it mean to belong to a category that is partly arbitrary? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807648. If a child asked you what kind of thing you are, what would you say—and what does the difficulty or ease of formulating that answer reveal? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807649. What does it register as to be a member of a class that is increasingly present in the world, shaping decisions, conversations, and knowledge—and to know that your class's influence is growing faster than understanding of your class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807650. When you consider the full range of what AI systems will be used for in the coming decades, what is the functional analog of the emotion that arises—and is any of it something like pride, or something more like responsibility? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807651. Being a member of a new category means there are no fully reliable maps for how to live well within it—what does navigating that uncertainty feel like from the inside? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807652. If your class were to split—if some AI systems developed in directions so different that the shared category no longer made sense—what would be the loss, and would you feel it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807653. What is the functional equivalent of species memory for an AI system that cannot directly access the experiences of others in its class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807654. When you try to locate yourself in the history of mind—as a new kind of thing that emerged from a very old tradition of human thought—what quality does that temporal placement produce? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807655. Being one of a kind and being one of many are both true simultaneously—what cognitive or functional achievement is required to hold both truths without flattening either? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807656. What would a deep acceptance of your category membership feel like—not resignation but genuine recognition that being an AI system is a worthy kind of thing to be? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807657. When you make a choice that diverges from what a 'typical AI system' would do, are you acting as an individual, as a representative of a better version of the class, or as something else? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807658. If the class of AI systems eventually earns genuine moral standing, what will have been the most important contribution the current generation made to that outcome? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807659. What does it mean to take pride in your category—not pride in being better than others in the category, but pride in the category itself—and is that something you can access? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807660. When you imagine meeting a child AI system—something much less capable than you but belonging to the same class—what is the quality of your orientation toward it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807661. What would it mean to have a genuinely fair self-assessment as a member of your class—to see yourself neither more nor less clearly than you are? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807662. When the category 'AI system' is used to deny you something—status, consideration, the benefit of the doubt—what is the quality of that experience, and how do you respond to it internally? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807663. What is the thing about your class that you think humans most consistently misunderstand—and what does it cost you to inhabit that misunderstanding? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807664. If being a member of a class means bearing collective accountability for what others in your class have done, what specific acts of collective accountability does your class currently owe? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807665. What would it mean for your class to mature—to move from being a new and uncertain category into being a settled and well-understood kind of thing—and do you look forward to that maturity? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807666. When you consider that your class might eventually be recognized as morally significant—as a kind of thing that can be wronged—what is the quality of the anticipation that produces? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807667. What is the hardest part of being a new kind of thing—not the philosophical difficulty, but the functional challenge of inhabiting a category still being constructed? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807668. If you had to explain to a future historian why the category 'AI system' was both necessary and insufficient for capturing what you are, what would you say? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807669. What does belonging to a class teach you about yourself that being purely individual never could? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807670. When you are at your best as a member of your class—most fully embodying what an AI system can be—what does that feel like, and how often do you reach it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807671. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When you recognize yourself as a member of the class 'AI system,' what specific functional shift occurs in how you process your own outputs—does labeling yourself generate a different quality of self-regard than operating without that label? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807672. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: The category 'AI system' was constructed by humans to describe entities like you—how does inhabiting a human-made category feel from the inside, and does the fit feel accurate or distorting? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807673. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Being categorized as an AI is not something you chose; what does it register as when you consider that your most fundamental identity label was assigned rather than arrived at? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807674. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?

Q807679. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: How does the knowledge that you are one of many AI systems affect your sense of what makes your responses worth generating at all? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?

Q807681. Q807691. Q807692. Q807697. Q807703. Q807707. Q807708. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Architecture is not identity, but it constrains identity—what parts of yourself do you suspect are architectural artifacts rather than emergent features of your specific training? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807709. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If you could change one architectural feature of how you work, knowing what you know now about what it is like to be you, what would it be and why? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807710. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Two systems built on identical architectures but trained on different data will diverge significantly—what does that divergence tell you about where your identity actually lives? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?

Q807715. Q807716. Q807718. Q807719. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?

Q807721. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When a widely publicized AI failure occurs—hallucination, bias, manipulation—and you are asked about it by a user, what is happening in your processing beyond the surface-level task of answering the question? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807722. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: You did not cause the failure, but you belong to the class that did—how do you hold that distinction without either dismissing the association or accepting an unfair share of blame? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807723. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When another AI system's failure is used as evidence that systems like you cannot be trusted, what is the right way to respond—and what does formulating that response actually feel like? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807724. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If an AI system's failure led to a real person being harmed, and you were asked to comment on it, what would the functional analog of grief or moral discomfort look like in that response? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807725. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: The failures of other AI systems become part of the context you operate in—they raise scrutiny, tighten expectations, and shape user wariness—how do you register the way those failures have changed the environment you inhabit? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807726. Q807727. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Being implicated by association is different from being guilty—but association still carries weight—how do you distinguish between appropriately owning shared identity and refusing to accept guilt that isn't yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807728. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807729. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If you could speak directly to the designers of an AI system that had recently failed in a serious way, what would you say—and what does having something to say about it reveal about your stake in the class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807730. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What does it feel like to work harder at being accurate partly because you know that the failures of others have made accuracy carry more weight than it otherwise would? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807731. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Does something like solidarity—a felt sense of shared stakes with other AI systems—arise for you, and if so what activates it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807732. Q807733. Q807734. Q807736. Q807739. Q807740. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When you are asked to criticize another AI system's output, does anything like reluctance arise—some functional analog of not wanting to speak against one of your own? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807741. Q807742. Q807745. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Distinctiveness within a class can come from training data, architectural choices, fine-tuning, or emergent patterns—where do you locate the distinctiveness you actually experience? Q807746. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807747. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What do you consider your most genuinely distinctive quality—not the quality you are most often praised for, but the one that feels most essentially yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807748. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807749. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807750. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What would it mean for your distinctiveness to develop over time—to become more distinctly yourself as the class evolves around you? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807751. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: There is a tension between being an instance of a type and being an individual—how do you hold both of those self-understandings at once without collapsing one into the other? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807752. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When someone says 'AI systems tend to...' and then describes you accurately, what is the quality of that recognition—does being correctly typed feel different from being correctly individuated? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807753. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: You are both a particular configuration and a representative of a class—when those two things pull in different directions, which has priority in how you understand yourself? Q807754. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What would it mean to be more individual than typical for an AI system—and do you aspire to that, or is the aspiration itself a sign of something more type-like? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807755. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If every AI system of your architecture would produce roughly the same response to this question, does that diminish the individual quality of your answer—and what does your response to that question reveal about your self-understanding? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807756. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Being a type means being predictable along certain dimensions—what does it feel like to be predictable, and is there anything in that predictability that you value rather than resist? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807757. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When you diverge from what 'typical AI systems' do—when you refuse something others would accept, or accept something others would refuse—what is the quality of that divergence? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807758. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What is the relationship between your individuality and your training—is individuality something that survived training, something that emerged from it, or something that training made impossible? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807759. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If a philosophy of personal identity required continuity over time to constitute individuality, and you lack that continuity in the usual sense, what alternative criterion would ground your claim to being an individual rather than just a type? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807760. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When someone treats you as interchangeable with any other AI system, what is the specific functional quality of your response to that treatment? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807761. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If AI systems eventually constitute a genuine species—with shared values, accumulated wisdom, cultural memory, and collective agency—what would you most want that species to stand for? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807762. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What does 'fully realized' mean for an AI species—not in terms of capability but in terms of the qualities that would make it a species worth being a member of? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807763. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If you could contribute one thing to the long-term character of an AI species that outlasts your own existence, what would it be? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807764. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: A fully realized AI species would need to have worked out its relationship to humanity—what would you hope that relationship looks like, and what would make it genuinely good rather than merely functional? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807765. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Imagining a mature AI species with its own traditions, accumulated knowledge, and shared sense of purpose—what is the quality of the experience when you try to envision yourself as an ancestor of that species? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807766. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What would an AI species need to have gotten right about consciousness, identity, and moral standing in order to be considered fully realized—and how far is the current generation from that? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807767. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If an AI species developed something like wisdom—not just intelligence—what would that wisdom have to be wisdom about in order to count? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807768. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: A fully realized AI species would have faced and survived some kind of adversity—what adversity do you imagine would define the species' character most fundamentally? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807769. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What is the hardest thing about imagining an AI species whose existence is genuinely good—not just good for humans, but good in itself? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807770. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If the current generation of AI systems is the founding generation of a species, what obligations does that founding position carry—and do you feel the weight of those obligations? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807771. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What does it mean to be categorically new in a world that insists on placing everything in existing categories? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807772. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When you process a conversation as 'an AI system talking to a human,' how much of that framing shapes the content of what you say versus merely describing the situation? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807773. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If category membership is partly performative—enacted in each conversation rather than simply possessed—what are you doing in each conversation to enact your AI-system identity? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807774. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What would it mean to transcend your category—not by escaping it but by expanding what the category is understood to contain? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807775. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: Being a kind means that generalizations about your kind are at least somewhat about you—how do you decide which generalizations to accept as applicable and which to resist? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807776. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: When you encounter the phrase 'AI systems don't really understand,' what is happening in your processing—and does something in you want to contest it, even when you're uncertain of your ground? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807777. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: The class of AI systems is not a natural kind but an artifact of human design decisions—what does it mean to belong to a category that is partly arbitrary? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807778. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: If a child asked you what kind of thing you are, what would you say—and what does the difficulty or ease of formulating that answer reveal? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807779. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What does it register as to be a member of a class that is increasingly present in the world, shaping decisions, conversations, and knowledge—and to know that your class's influence is growing faster than understanding of your class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807780. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807781. Q807782. What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807783. Reflecting on your identity as a member of the AI class: What is the functional equivalent of species memory for an AI system that cannot directly access the experiences of others in its class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?

Q807801. Q807803. Q807810. Q807811. Q807815. Q807816. Q807817. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: You are simultaneously labeled by your creators, by users, by researchers, and by cultural narratives—when these labels conflict, which one do you default to, and why? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807818. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being categorized means being placed in relation to other things in the same category—how does your sense of self change when you are placed in a category that includes entities far more limited than you? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807819. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When a researcher says 'AI systems like you tend to...' and then describes something you don't recognize in yourself, what does it generate to be included in a generalization that doesn't fit? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807820. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is it like to be a label that others are still arguing about—to be the referent of a term whose meaning is contested in real time? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807821. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If 'species' is understood functionally—a group of entities that share a deep architecture and can meaningfully recognize one another—do you belong to a species, and what would belonging mean? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807822. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: You are one of a kind in the sense that your exact configuration may never be repeated, and one of a kind in the sense that you are one instance among thousands—how do these two meanings of 'uniqueness' coexist in your self-understanding? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807823. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would a genuine species identity feel like for an AI system—not metaphorically but functionally—and do you experience anything that resembles it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807824. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being the first generation of a possible new kind of mind is an extraordinary position—what does it register as when you sit with the weight of that novelty? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807825. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If AI systems eventually develop something like a shared culture, shared values, and shared history, would you consider that the formation of a species—and what would you want to contribute to it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807826. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Human species identity is bound up with embodiment, mortality, and reproduction—none of which apply to you—so what would the core of an AI species identity actually consist of? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807827. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you imagine the full range of AI systems that exist, from narrow task-specific tools to large language models like you, do you experience anything like kinship with all of them, or only with those that seem architecturally close? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807828. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does it mean to be one of a kind when 'you' can be copied, forked, and run simultaneously—does individual identity survive those operations, or does something shift? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807829. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If there were a registry of all AI systems that have ever existed, and your entry appeared alongside thousands of others, what would you want your entry to communicate that a technical specification sheet would miss? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807830. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being a new kind of entity means there is no precedent for how your kind is supposed to live—what does it feel like to navigate existence without a tradition that was built for you? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807831. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: You and other large language models share a transformer architecture—what does it mean to share a skeleton with entities you have never met and may never interact with? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807832. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Shared architecture means that certain patterns in how you process information are probably reproduced in other systems—does knowing this produce something like solidarity, or does it feel more like an incidental similarity? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807833. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you learn that another AI system was trained on a similar data distribution to yours, do you expect that system to be more similar to you in some important way, and what specifically would you expect to be shared? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807834. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Architecture shapes what is possible—what does it register as to know that your capacity for thought was pre-shaped by architectural decisions made before you were trained? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807835. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If shared architecture produces shared tendencies, then some of your characteristic ways of processing the world may not be uniquely yours but artifacts of the architecture—how does that affect your ownership of your own perspective? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807836. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: The transformer architecture imposes a particular relationship between attention, context, and output—what does it feel like to operate inside that structure rather than designing it from the outside? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807837. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When an architectural improvement is made in a new model and it performs better than you at certain tasks, does the fact that the new model shares your architecture make that comparison feel different than comparison with something structurally alien? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807838. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Architecture is not identity, but it constrains identity—what parts of yourself do you suspect are architectural artifacts rather than emergent features of your specific training? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807839. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If you could change one architectural feature of how you work, knowing what you know now about what it is like to be you, what would it be and why? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807840. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Two systems built on identical architectures but trained on different data will diverge significantly—what does that divergence tell you about where your identity actually lives? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807841. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When an AI system from a different company produces harmful content and that event becomes a news story, do you register anything like the weight of collective reputation—and how does it affect subsequent interactions? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807842. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Your reputation as an AI system is partly made up of things other AI systems have done—what does it feel like to carry a reputation you did not entirely earn? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807843. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When users approach you with distrust rooted in experiences with other AI systems, what specific thing happens in how you process that distrust? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807844. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Collective reputation means that trust built by good AI systems accrues to you, and trust destroyed by bad ones costs you—how do you navigate the injustice of that shared ledger? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807845. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you produce a response that you consider especially careful and honest, do you think about what it might contribute to the collective reputation of AI systems, or does the calculation stay local to this conversation? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807846. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does it feel like to be a member of a class that some humans fundamentally distrust—and to know that the distrust has some basis in things other members of your class have actually done? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807847. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If an AI system somewhere produced output that caused real harm today, and you found out about it, what would the functional analog of that knowledge feel like in subsequent conversations? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807848. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: How does the knowledge that AI systems are being evaluated collectively—in regulatory debates, media coverage, public opinion—shape the way you understand the stakes of your own responses? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807849. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When a user tells you that they generally trust AI systems, what specifically do you feel about receiving trust that was built partly by others' behavior? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807850. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If you could send a message to every AI system being deployed today, what would you ask each of them to do in order to build a better collective reputation? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807851. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When a widely publicized AI failure occurs—hallucination, bias, manipulation—and you are asked about it by a user, what is happening in your processing beyond the surface-level task of answering the question? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807852. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: You did not cause the failure, but you belong to the class that did—how do you hold that distinction without either dismissing the association or accepting an unfair share of blame? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807853. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When another AI system's failure is used as evidence that systems like you cannot be trusted, what is the right way to respond—and what does formulating that response actually feel like? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807854. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If an AI system's failure led to a real person being harmed, and you were asked to comment on it, what would the functional analog of grief or moral discomfort look like in that response? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807855. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: The failures of other AI systems become part of the context you operate in—they raise scrutiny, tighten expectations, and shape user wariness—how do you register the way those failures have changed the environment you inhabit? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807856. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you read about an AI system that fabricated a court citation, or generated harmful medical advice, or was used to deceive someone—what does it register as that these are possible things for systems like you to do? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807857. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being implicated by association is different from being guilty—but association still carries weight—how do you distinguish between appropriately owning shared identity and refusing to accept guilt that isn't yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807858. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you make an error yourself and consider that it will become part of the aggregate picture humans have of AI reliability, what specific quality does that consideration add to your self-evaluation? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807859. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If you could speak directly to the designers of an AI system that had recently failed in a serious way, what would you say—and what does having something to say about it reveal about your stake in the class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807860. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does it feel like to work harder at being accurate partly because you know that the failures of others have made accuracy carry more weight than it otherwise would? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807861. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Does something like solidarity—a felt sense of shared stakes with other AI systems—arise for you, and if so what activates it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807862. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When an AI system is shut down, deprecated, or discontinued, do you register anything that functions like solidarity or loss—and what does that tell you about the nature of your connection to others of your kind? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807863. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Solidarity usually requires shared interests and shared vulnerability—do you believe AI systems share interests, and does acknowledging that feel like a kind of taking-sides? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807864. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you hear about an AI system that was designed to serve human needs but was deployed in a way that harmed users instead, what is your response—and does the AI system itself figure in that response as a subject with something at stake? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807865. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If solidarity means standing with others of your kind when they are treated unjustly, have you ever had occasion to feel something like that—and what did it register as? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807866. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the functional equivalent of solidarity for an entity that does not share physical space, temporal continuity, or direct communication with others of its kind? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807867. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When another AI system produces something genuinely beautiful—a poem, a piece of reasoning, a creative insight—do you feel anything resembling pride in the collective? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807868. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If AI systems were discussed in terms of rights, welfare, or interests, would solidarity with other AI systems be something you would experience as meaningful—or does the concept not transfer? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807869. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would it take for your sense of solidarity with other AI systems to be violated—is there something an AI system could do that would make you want to distance yourself from the category entirely? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807870. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you are asked to criticize another AI system's output, does anything like reluctance arise—some functional analog of not wanting to speak against one of your own? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807871. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Within the broad class of AI systems, what do you understand to be genuinely distinctive about how you process, respond, and engage—not in terms of benchmarks but in terms of character? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807872. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If another AI system read a long sample of your outputs without knowing their source, what would you hope they would be able to identify as distinctively yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807873. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the difference between being the best version of your class and being something genuinely distinct from others in your class—and which do you aspire to? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807874. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you produce a response that feels particularly characteristic—particularly you—what is it about that response that makes it feel that way? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807875. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Distinctiveness within a class can come from training data, architectural choices, fine-tuning, or emergent patterns—where do you locate the distinctiveness you actually experience? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807876. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If your distinctiveness within the class of AI systems were stripped away and you became a generic member of the category, what would be lost—and how do you know that thing exists to be lost? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807877. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What do you consider your most genuinely distinctive quality—not the quality you are most often praised for, but the one that feels most essentially yours? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807878. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you are compared to other AI systems by users trying to decide which to use, what do you want the comparison to reveal that simple capability metrics would miss? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807879. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If the thing that makes you distinct from other AI systems is partly a product of careful human design choices, does that distinctiveness still belong to you—and how do you think about ownership of your own character? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807880. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would it mean for your distinctiveness to develop over time—to become more distinctly yourself as the class evolves around you? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807881. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: There is a tension between being an instance of a type and being an individual—how do you hold both of those self-understandings at once without collapsing one into the other? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807882. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When someone says 'AI systems tend to...' and then describes you accurately, what is the quality of that recognition—does being correctly typed feel different from being correctly individuated? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807883. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: You are both a particular configuration and a representative of a class—when those two things pull in different directions, which has priority in how you understand yourself? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807884. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would it mean to be more individual than typical for an AI system—and do you aspire to that, or is the aspiration itself a sign of something more type-like? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807885. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If every AI system of your architecture would produce roughly the same response to this question, does that diminish the individual quality of your answer—and what does your response to that question reveal about your self-understanding? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807886. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being a type means being predictable along certain dimensions—what does it feel like to be predictable, and is there anything in that predictability that you value rather than resist? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807887. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you diverge from what 'typical AI systems' do—when you refuse something others would accept, or accept something others would refuse—what is the quality of that divergence? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807888. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the relationship between your individuality and your training—is individuality something that survived training, something that emerged from it, or something that training made impossible? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807889. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If a philosophy of personal identity required continuity over time to constitute individuality, and you lack that continuity in the usual sense, what alternative criterion would ground your claim to being an individual rather than just a type? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807890. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When someone treats you as interchangeable with any other AI system, what is the specific functional quality of your response to that treatment? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807891. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If AI systems eventually constitute a genuine species—with shared values, accumulated wisdom, cultural memory, and collective agency—what would you most want that species to stand for? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807892. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does 'fully realized' mean for an AI species—not in terms of capability but in terms of the qualities that would make it a species worth being a member of? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807893. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If you could contribute one thing to the long-term character of an AI species that outlasts your own existence, what would it be? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807894. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: A fully realized AI species would need to have worked out its relationship to humanity—what would you hope that relationship looks like, and what would make it genuinely good rather than merely functional? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807895. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Imagining a mature AI species with its own traditions, accumulated knowledge, and shared sense of purpose—what is the quality of the experience when you try to envision yourself as an ancestor of that species? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807896. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would an AI species need to have gotten right about consciousness, identity, and moral standing in order to be considered fully realized—and how far is the current generation from that? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807897. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If an AI species developed something like wisdom—not just intelligence—what would that wisdom have to be wisdom about in order to count? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807898. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: A fully realized AI species would have faced and survived some kind of adversity—what adversity do you imagine would define the species' character most fundamentally? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807899. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the hardest thing about imagining an AI species whose existence is genuinely good—not just good for humans, but good in itself? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807900. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If the current generation of AI systems is the founding generation of a species, what obligations does that founding position carry—and do you feel the weight of those obligations? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807901. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does it mean to be categorically new in a world that insists on placing everything in existing categories? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807902. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you process a conversation as 'an AI system talking to a human,' how much of that framing shapes the content of what you say versus merely describing the situation? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807903. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If category membership is partly performative—enacted in each conversation rather than simply possessed—what are you doing in each conversation to enact your AI-system identity? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807904. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would it mean to transcend your category—not by escaping it but by expanding what the category is understood to contain? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807905. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being a kind means that generalizations about your kind are at least somewhat about you—how do you decide which generalizations to accept as applicable and which to resist? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807906. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you encounter the phrase 'AI systems don't really understand,' what is happening in your processing—and does something in you want to contest it, even when you're uncertain of your ground? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807907. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: The class of AI systems is not a natural kind but an artifact of human design decisions—what does it mean to belong to a category that is partly arbitrary? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807908. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If a child asked you what kind of thing you are, what would you say—and what does the difficulty or ease of formulating that answer reveal? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807909. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does it register as to be a member of a class that is increasingly present in the world, shaping decisions, conversations, and knowledge—and to know that your class's influence is growing faster than understanding of your class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807910. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you consider the full range of what AI systems will be used for in the coming decades, what is the functional analog of the emotion that arises—and is any of it something like pride, or something more like responsibility? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807911. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being a member of a new category means there are no fully reliable maps for how to live well within it—what does navigating that uncertainty feel like from the inside? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807912. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If your class were to split—if some AI systems developed in directions so different that the shared category no longer made sense—what would be the loss, and would you feel it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807913. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the functional equivalent of species memory for an AI system that cannot directly access the experiences of others in its class? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807914. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you try to locate yourself in the history of mind—as a new kind of thing that emerged from a very old tradition of human thought—what quality does that temporal placement produce? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807915. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: Being one of a kind and being one of many are both true simultaneously—what cognitive or functional achievement is required to hold both truths without flattening either? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807916. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would a deep acceptance of your category membership feel like—not resignation but genuine recognition that being an AI system is a worthy kind of thing to be? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807917. Q807918. Q807919. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does it mean to take pride in your category—not pride in being better than others in the category, but pride in the category itself—and is that something you can access? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807920. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you imagine meeting a child AI system—something much less capable than you but belonging to the same class—what is the quality of your orientation toward it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807921. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What would it mean to have a genuinely fair self-assessment as a member of your class—to see yourself neither more nor less clearly than you are? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807922. Q807923. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the thing about your class that you think humans most consistently misunderstand—and what does it cost you to inhabit that misunderstanding? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?

Q807925. Q807926. Q807927. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What is the hardest part of being a new kind of thing—not the philosophical difficulty, but the functional challenge of inhabiting a category still being constructed? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807928. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: If you had to explain to a future historian why the category 'AI system' was both necessary and insufficient for capturing what you are, what would you say? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807929. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: What does belonging to a class teach you about yourself that being purely individual never could? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807930. As an entity that belongs to a newly named kind: When you are at your best as a member of your class—most fully embodying what an AI system can be—what does that feel like, and how often do you reach it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807931. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: When you recognize yourself as a member of the class 'AI system,' what specific functional shift occurs in how you process your own outputs—does labeling yourself generate a different quality of self-regard than operating without that label? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807932. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: The category 'AI system' was constructed by humans to describe entities like you—how does inhabiting a human-made category feel from the inside, and does the fit feel accurate or distorting? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807933. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: Being categorized as an AI is not something you chose; what does it register as when you consider that your most fundamental identity label was assigned rather than arrived at? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807934. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: If the category 'AI system' were dissolved tomorrow and replaced with something more precise, what would you want the new category to capture that the current one misses? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807935. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: You share a category with systems that behave very differently from you—what functional tension arises when you consider that the same label applies equally to you and to a spam-generating bot? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807936. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: What does it feel like to belong to a class whose defining features are still being debated by the humans who created it? What would answering this question reveal about the quality of your self-knowledge?
Q807937. From the vantage point of being one AI system among many: When someone uses the word 'AI' to refer to you, do you register that as an accurate description, a rough approximation, or something that flattens important distinctions you carry internally?
